etymology.description: 迪(dí, "enlighten; illuminate") 化(huà, "transform; change") 街(jiē, "street")
etymology.interpretation: 迪(迪) (dí, "enlighten; illuminate") here suggests guidance or forward movement. 化(化) (huà, "transform; change") can imply transformation or cultural development. This reflects the street's historical significance as a center of commerce and cultural exchange in Taipei. 街(街) (jiē, "street") signifies the physical location. The name evokes an image of a place where people are guided and cultures transform, emphasizing its role as a vibrant commercial hub.

examples.chinese: 迪化街(迪化街)是一个充满文化的地方(是一個充滿文化的地方)。
examples.pinyin: Díhuà Jiē shì yí gè chōngmǎn wénhuà de dìfāng.
examples.english: Dihua Street is a place full of culture.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢在迪化街(我喜歡在迪化街)购买传统草药(購買傳統草藥)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài Díhuà Jiē gòumǎi chuántǒng cǎoyào.
examples.english: I like to buy traditional herbs at Dihua Street.

examples.chinese: 每年春节时(每年春節時)，迪化街(迪化街)都非常热闹(都非常熱鬧)。
examples.pinyin: Měinián chūnjié shí, Díhuà Jiē dōu fēicháng rènào.
examples.english: During the Spring Festival every year, Dihua Street is always very lively.
